message:
fix(fr-store): GETRANGE with fully-negative range returns clamped slice [bd-frankenredis-grangneg]

fr-store::getrange only clamped the start offset to 0 after the
negative-offset normalization, leaving end negative when both
inputs were negative AND large in magnitude (e.g. start=-100,
end=-90 on a length-6 string yields s=clamp(-94)=0, e=-84). The
's > e' guard then wrongly returned empty instead of the upstream
clamped-to-[0..=0] slice.

Upstream t_string.c::getrangeCommand clamps both offsets the same
way; mirror that here.

Verified byte-for-byte against vendored Redis 7.2.4: 10 negative-
offset edge cases (incl. -100..-90, -200..-150, -3..-3) now match.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
